course.title: Modern TypeScript 7: Contracts to Production
course.description: JavaScript developers who need rigorous TypeScript reasoning from first inference through safe public library release. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate typescript 7 language, compiler, configuration, and migration behavior through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate inference, narrowing, object and function types, generics, classes, type transforms, modules, declarations, runtime validation, async work, testing, and release through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate four cumulative stakeholder projects and an independent performance exam through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

competencies.statement: Distinguish compile-time type evidence from emitted JavaScript and runtime validation.
misconceptions: A passing type check proves every runtime input is valid.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish compile-time type evidence from emitted JavaScript and runtime validation.

competencies.statement: Predict which TypeScript constructs erase, transform, or affect emitted JavaScript and source maps.
misconceptions: Type annotations remain available as runtime metadata.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict which TypeScript constructs erase, transform, or affect emitted JavaScript and source maps.

competencies.statement: Use the TypeScript 7 native compiler and explain when a TypeScript 6 API compatibility tool remains necessary.
misconceptions: TypeScript 7 exposes the same programmatic compiler API as TypeScript 6.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use the TypeScript 7 native compiler and explain when a TypeScript 6 API compatibility tool remains necessary.

competencies.statement: Read diagnostic codes, primary locations, related information, and causal type relationships before changing code.
misconceptions: The first diagnostic is always the root cause.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Read diagnostic codes, primary locations, related information, and causal type relationships before changing code.

competencies.statement: Record compiler, runtime, configuration, dependency, and module conditions needed to reproduce a type result.
misconceptions: Editor success guarantees the clean command-line build uses the same project.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record compiler, runtime, configuration, dependency, and module conditions needed to reproduce a type result.

competencies.statement: Predict best-common, contextual, return, and evolving inference from the surrounding expression and use site.
misconceptions: TypeScript infers the narrowest possible type in every context.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict best-common, contextual, return, and evolving inference from the surrounding expression and use site.

competencies.statement: Add annotations at public, recursive, overloaded, and external boundaries without redundantly annotating obvious locals.
misconceptions: More annotations always make a program safer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Add annotations at public, recursive, overloaded, and external boundaries without redundantly annotating obvious locals.

competencies.statement: Control literal widening with const bindings, readonly context, const assertions, and const type parameters.
misconceptions: A const variable always has a literal type for every nested property.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Control literal widening with const bindings, readonly context, const assertions, and const type parameters.

competencies.statement: Use satisfies to check a value against a contract while retaining useful inferred literal information.
misconceptions: Satisfies changes the runtime value into the target type.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use satisfies to check a value against a contract while retaining useful inferred literal information.

competencies.statement: Use lowercase primitive types and distinguish them from boxed wrapper object types.
misconceptions: String and string are interchangeable type spellings.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use lowercase primitive types and distinguish them from boxed wrapper object types.

competencies.statement: Use only operations valid for every union member until evidence supports narrowing.
misconceptions: A union value exposes all properties from every member.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use only operations valid for every union member until evidence supports narrowing.

competencies.statement: Choose unknown, any, and never from trust, escape-hatch, and impossibility semantics.
misconceptions: Unknown and any permit the same unchecked operations.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose unknown, any, and never from trust, escape-hatch, and impossibility semantics.

competencies.statement: Narrow with typeof, equality, truthiness, in, instanceof, and discriminant checks while respecting edge cases.
misconceptions: A truthiness check safely removes only missing values.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Narrow with typeof, equality, truthiness, in, instanceof, and discriminant checks while respecting edge cases.

competencies.statement: Write boolean predicates and type predicates whose runtime checks actually justify their claimed types.
misconceptions: A type-predicate return annotation makes an incomplete check true.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write boolean predicates and type predicates whose runtime checks actually justify their claimed types.

competencies.statement: Use assertion functions only when failure behavior and the asserted runtime evidence are explicit.
misconceptions: An assertion signature performs validation by itself.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use assertion functions only when failure behavior and the asserted runtime evidence are explicit.

competencies.statement: Model required, optional, readonly, indexed, and exact-optional property behavior under strict configuration.
misconceptions: An optional property is always identical to a required property whose value includes undefined.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model required, optional, readonly, indexed, and exact-optional property behavior under strict configuration.

competencies.statement: Choose interface or type alias from extension, unions, declaration merging, diagnostics, and API intent.
misconceptions: Interfaces are for objects while type aliases cannot describe objects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose interface or type alias from extension, unions, declaration merging, diagnostics, and API intent.

competencies.statement: Predict structural assignability while identifying where extra members, methods, and variance create risk.
misconceptions: Matching a type name is required for assignment.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict structural assignability while identifying where extra members, methods, and variance create risk.

competencies.statement: Explain fresh object literal checks and preserve validation after values pass through variables or spreads.
misconceptions: Excess-property checks reject extra keys in every assignment path.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain fresh object literal checks and preserve validation after values pass through variables or spreads.

competencies.statement: Distinguish readonly property, readonly collection, const assertion, and deep immutable domain policy.
misconceptions: Readonly recursively freezes runtime objects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish readonly property, readonly collection, const assertion, and deep immutable domain policy.

competencies.statement: Model parameter, return, callback, this, and side-effect expectations with readable function types.
misconceptions: A void callback cannot return any runtime value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model parameter, return, callback, this, and side-effect expectations with readable function types.

competencies.statement: Reason about callback parameter compatibility under strictFunctionTypes without accepting unsound narrower handlers.
misconceptions: Function parameters are always covariant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reason about callback parameter compatibility under strictFunctionTypes without accepting unsound narrower handlers.

competencies.statement: Model optional, default, rest, tuple-rest, and destructured parameters without ambiguous call shapes.
misconceptions: Optional and default parameters have identical call and runtime behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model optional, default, rest, tuple-rest, and destructured parameters without ambiguous call shapes.

competencies.statement: Write visible overload signatures with one compatible implementation and prefer unions when return relationships do not vary.
misconceptions: Callers can use the implementation signature of an overloaded function.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write visible overload signatures with one compatible implementation and prefer unions when return relationships do not vary.

competencies.statement: Represent callbacks, thrown failures, and unknown catch values without pretending exceptions appear in return types.
misconceptions: A function returning void cannot throw.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent callbacks, thrown failures, and unknown catch values without pretending exceptions appear in return types.

competencies.statement: Introduce a type parameter only when multiple positions share a relationship that callers need preserved.
misconceptions: Every reusable function needs a generic type parameter.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Introduce a type parameter only when multiple positions share a relationship that callers need preserved.

competencies.statement: Constrain operations while returning the caller-specific subtype rather than widening to the constraint.
misconceptions: A constrained generic always returns exactly the constraint type.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Constrain operations while returning the caller-specific subtype rather than widening to the constraint.

competencies.statement: Use keyof and indexed access types to connect property names with their corresponding value types.
misconceptions: Keyof an object always produces string.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use keyof and indexed access types to connect property names with their corresponding value types.

competencies.statement: Predict inference candidates, defaults, contextual inference, and when an explicit type argument hides a design problem.
misconceptions: Type arguments should always be written explicitly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict inference candidates, defaults, contextual inference, and when an explicit type argument hides a design problem.

competencies.statement: Analyze covariance, contravariance, invariance, and bivariant compatibility from producer and consumer positions.
misconceptions: All generic containers are covariant because their element types are related.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Analyze covariance, contravariance, invariance, and bivariant compatibility from producer and consumer positions.

competencies.statement: Distinguish instance-side and static-side class types, fields, methods, and constructor signatures.
misconceptions: The class name describes only its constructor object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish instance-side and static-side class types, fields, methods, and constructor signatures.

competencies.statement: Use public, protected, TypeScript private, and JavaScript hash-private fields with their actual compile-time and runtime boundaries.
misconceptions: The private keyword creates the same runtime privacy as a hash field.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use public, protected, TypeScript private, and JavaScript hash-private fields with their actual compile-time and runtime boundaries.

competencies.statement: Use parameter properties only when concise construction does not hide validation or ownership transitions.
misconceptions: Every constructor parameter should be a parameter property.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use parameter properties only when concise construction does not hide validation or ownership transitions.

competencies.statement: Use abstract classes and implements clauses while recognizing that implementation checks do not change inferred member types.
misconceptions: Implements copies the interface members and runtime validation into the class.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use abstract classes and implements clauses while recognizing that implementation checks do not change inferred member types.

competencies.statement: Choose composition or inheritance from substitution, state ownership, variation axes, and test isolation.
misconceptions: Typing makes deep inheritance safe by default.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose composition or inheritance from substitution, state ownership, variation axes, and test isolation.

competencies.statement: Use type-context typeof to derive from runtime declarations without confusing it with runtime typeof.
misconceptions: Type-context typeof executes the referenced value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use type-context typeof to derive from runtime declarations without confusing it with runtime typeof.

competencies.statement: Map over property keys with modifiers, key remapping, and value transforms while preserving intended optionality.
misconceptions: A mapped type iterates over runtime object properties.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map over property keys with modifiers, key remapping, and value transforms while preserving intended optionality.

competencies.statement: Use distributive and non-distributive conditional types with explicit true and false branch meaning.
misconceptions: Every conditional type distributes over every input.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use distributive and non-distributive conditional types with explicit true and false branch meaning.

competencies.statement: Use infer inside conditional patterns to extract relationships without turning readable APIs into type puzzles.
misconceptions: Infer can declare a type variable anywhere in a type alias.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use infer inside conditional patterns to extract relationships without turning readable APIs into type puzzles.

competencies.statement: Build bounded template literal contracts while accounting for Unicode, combinatorial growth, and runtime validation.
misconceptions: A template literal type parses and validates runtime strings automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build bounded template literal contracts while accounting for Unicode, combinatorial growth, and runtime validation.

competencies.statement: Design variants with stable literal discriminants and variant-specific required data.
misconceptions: Several optional properties express states as safely as a discriminated union.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design variants with stable literal discriminants and variant-specific required data.

competencies.statement: Use never assignments or exhaustive helpers to make newly added variants produce actionable diagnostics.
misconceptions: A default branch automatically proves every union member was handled.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use never assignments or exhaustive helpers to make newly added variants produce actionable diagnostics.

competencies.statement: Represent expected domain failure as typed result variants while preserving unexpected exceptions.
misconceptions: Every exception should be converted into a success-or-error union.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent expected domain failure as typed result variants while preserving unexpected exceptions.

competencies.statement: Constrain legal state transitions and outputs without permitting impossible intermediate combinations.
misconceptions: If each state is typed, every transition between them is automatically legal.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Constrain legal state transitions and outputs without permitting impossible intermediate combinations.

competencies.statement: Add, deprecate, and migrate variants with compatibility tests and consumer impact evidence.
misconceptions: Adding a union member is always backward compatible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Add, deprecate, and migrate variants with compatibility tests and consumer impact evidence.

competencies.statement: Choose bundler or NodeNext resolution and trace file, extension, package exports, and type lookup decisions.
misconceptions: Module resolution only searches for a matching source filename.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose bundler or NodeNext resolution and trace file, extension, package exports, and type lookup decisions.

competencies.statement: Distinguish type-only and value imports and preserve runtime side effects intentionally.
misconceptions: Import type and ordinary import always emit identical JavaScript.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish type-only and value imports and preserve runtime side effects intentionally.

competencies.statement: Align package exports, imports, type, declaration paths, runtime format, and supported environments.
misconceptions: A types field alone makes every package subpath typed and loadable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Align package exports, imports, type, declaration paths, runtime format, and supported environments.

competencies.statement: Author or inspect declaration files that describe real runtime values without implementations or false guarantees.
misconceptions: A declaration file supplies the missing runtime implementation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Author or inspect declaration files that describe real runtime values without implementations or false guarantees.

competencies.statement: Use augmentation or declaration merging only for owned, documented extension points with load-order evidence.
misconceptions: Module augmentation can create any new runtime export.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use augmentation or declaration merging only for owned, documented extension points with load-order evidence.

competencies.statement: Use strict, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, useUnknownInCatchVariables, and override checks from actual risk.
misconceptions: The strict flag enables every possible safety option.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use strict, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, useUnknownInCatchVariables, and override checks from actual risk.

competencies.statement: Account for TypeScript 7 strict, target, module, rootDir, types, and side-effect import defaults without legacy no-op flags.
misconceptions: A TypeScript 5 configuration behaves identically under TypeScript 7.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Account for TypeScript 7 strict, target, module, rootDir, types, and side-effect import defaults without legacy no-op flags.

competencies.statement: Predict root files from files, include, exclude, imports, types, and library selection.
misconceptions: Exclude prevents an imported file from joining the program.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict root files from files, include, exclude, imports, types, and library selection.

competencies.statement: Split projects with composite references, explicit outputs, graph ordering, and incremental clean-build evidence.
misconceptions: Project references are ordinary path aliases.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Split projects with composite references, explicit outputs, graph ordering, and incremental clean-build evidence.

competencies.statement: Use showConfig, traceResolution, explainFiles, clean builds, and fixed parallelism to diagnose configuration rather than guess.
misconceptions: Deleting random cache files is the first reliable configuration repair.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use showConfig, traceResolution, explainFiles, clean builds, and fixed parallelism to diagnose configuration rather than guess.

competencies.statement: Receive external JSON, storage, DOM, environment, and message data as unknown until evidence narrows it.
misconceptions: A generic JSON request helper can know the server response type without validation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Receive external JSON, storage, DOM, environment, and message data as unknown until evidence narrows it.

competencies.statement: Build parsers that return typed values or structured failure paths rather than booleans that discard evidence.
misconceptions: A type guard is always a complete parser.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build parsers that return typed values or structured failure paths rather than booleans that discard evidence.

competencies.statement: Handle required, optional, deprecated, defaulted, and versioned fields with changed-schema fixtures.
misconceptions: Making a field optional is always a harmless compatibility change.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle required, optional, deprecated, defaulted, and versioned fields with changed-schema fixtures.

competencies.statement: Use brands or refined constructors only after runtime validation establishes the represented invariant.
misconceptions: A brand prevents invalid values from entering through assertions or JavaScript.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use brands or refined constructors only after runtime validation establishes the represented invariant.

competencies.statement: Locate and replace as, non-null, double assertions, any, and suppression directives with evidence-producing boundaries.
misconceptions: Type assertions convert and validate runtime values.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Locate and replace as, non-null, double assertions, any, and suppression directives with evidence-producing boundaries.

competencies.statement: Model promise fulfillment values and rejection handling without pretending rejected types are encoded in Promise.
misconceptions: Promise has a second generic parameter for its rejection type.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model promise fulfillment values and rejection handling without pretending rejected types are encoded in Promise.

competencies.statement: Predict async return inference and retain discriminated result relationships across awaits.
misconceptions: An async function can directly return a non-Promise type annotation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict async return inference and retain discriminated result relationships across awaits.

competencies.statement: Propagate AbortSignal and distinguish cancellation, timeout, transport, parsing, and domain failures.
misconceptions: A timeout wrapper automatically cancels its underlying work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Propagate AbortSignal and distinguish cancellation, timeout, transport, parsing, and domain failures.

competencies.statement: Narrow event targets and DOM queries without non-null assertions or unsound global casts.
misconceptions: An event target always has the properties of the element receiving the handler.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Narrow event targets and DOM queries without non-null assertions or unsound global casts.

competencies.statement: Select DOM, worker, iterable, and server type libraries that match the real runtime without leaking incompatible globals.
misconceptions: Including every lib makes a project support every runtime.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select DOM, worker, iterable, and server type libraries that match the real runtime without leaking incompatible globals.

competencies.statement: Separate runtime behavior tests from compile-time assignability, inference, and expected-error tests.
misconceptions: A type test proves the emitted JavaScript behaves correctly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate runtime behavior tests from compile-time assignability, inference, and expected-error tests.

competencies.statement: Write stable rejected-usage tests that fail when diagnostics disappear or move for the wrong reason.
misconceptions: A ts-expect-error comment is acceptable without explaining the expected failure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write stable rejected-usage tests that fail when diagnostics disappear or move for the wrong reason.

competencies.statement: Build changed-schema, invalid-value, async, and clean-consumer fixtures around public boundaries.
misconceptions: Testing only well-typed calls covers runtime JavaScript consumers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build changed-schema, invalid-value, async, and clean-consumer fixtures around public boundaries.

competencies.statement: Use rename, references, deprecation, declaration diffs, and consumer builds to bound a refactor.
misconceptions: No local diagnostics prove a public refactor is safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use rename, references, deprecation, declaration diffs, and consumer builds to bound a refactor.

competencies.statement: Replace clever types when diagnostics, performance, or maintenance cost exceeds their safety value.
misconceptions: The most precise possible type is always the best API.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Replace clever types when diagnostics, performance, or maintenance cost exceeds their safety value.

competencies.statement: Measure cold and warm checks, project graphs, declarations, memory, and parallelism before changing configuration.
misconceptions: Type-level cleverness has no compiler performance cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure cold and warm checks, project graphs, declarations, memory, and parallelism before changing configuration.

competencies.statement: Migrate JavaScript incrementally with checkJs, JSDoc, allowJs, boundary validation, and shrinking suppression budgets.
misconceptions: Renaming every file to ts completes a safe migration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Migrate JavaScript incrementally with checkJs, JSDoc, allowJs, boundary validation, and shrinking suppression budgets.

competencies.statement: Remove TypeScript 6 deprecations, compare TS6 and TS7 diagnostics, and document temporary API-tool compatibility.
misconceptions: TypeScript 7 accepts every legacy compiler option and plugin API unchanged.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Remove TypeScript 6 deprecations, compare TS6 and TS7 diagnostics, and document temporary API-tool compatibility.

competencies.statement: Design minimum expressive public types, declarations, errors, and compatibility policy around user tasks.
misconceptions: Exporting every inferred implementation type makes an API easiest to use.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design minimum expressive public types, declarations, errors, and compatibility policy around user tasks.

competencies.statement: Verify clean install, strict build, tests, declarations, package exports, runtime smoke use, audit, rollback, and consumer acceptance.
misconceptions: Publishing successfully proves consumers can load and use the package.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify clean install, strict build, tests, declarations, package exports, runtime smoke use, audit, rollback, and consumer acceptance.

credential.title: Modern TypeScript 7: Contracts to Production Certificate

modules.title: TypeScript, JavaScript, and the TypeScript 7 Toolchain
modules.description: Trace a service-status module through TypeScript 7 checking, JavaScript emission, module loading, and runtime behavior. Learners cumulatively produce a compiler-to-runtime evidence notebook through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish compile-time type evidence from emitted JavaScript and runtime validation.
Predict which TypeScript constructs erase, transform, or affect emitted JavaScript and source maps.
Use the TypeScript 7 native compiler and explain when a TypeScript 6 API compatibility tool remains necessary.

modules.title: Inference, Annotations, Literals, and Widening
modules.description: Model service categories, thresholds, identifiers, and configuration without widening away domain meaning. Learners cumulatively produce a literal-safe service configuration model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Predict best-common, contextual, return, and evolving inference from the surrounding expression and use site.
Add annotations at public, recursive, overloaded, and external boundaries without redundantly annotating obvious locals.
Control literal widening with const bindings, readonly context, const assertions, and const type parameters.

modules.title: Unions, Unknown Values, and Control-Flow Narrowing
modules.description: Convert an unknown status feed into trusted records using evidence-producing guards and exhaustive decisions. Learners cumulatively produce an unknown-to-domain validation pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use only operations valid for every union member until evidence supports narrowing.
Choose unknown, any, and never from trust, escape-hatch, and impossibility semantics.
Narrow with typeof, equality, truthiness, in, instanceof, and discriminant checks while respecting edge cases.

modules.title: Object Types, Interfaces, Aliases, and Structural Compatibility
modules.description: Design public service records that evolve without silently accepting misspelled, mutable, or incompatible fields. Learners cumulatively produce an evolvable service-record contract library through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model required, optional, readonly, indexed, and exact-optional property behavior under strict configuration.
Choose interface or type alias from extension, unions, declaration merging, diagnostics, and API intent.
Predict structural assignability while identifying where extra members, methods, and variance create risk.

modules.title: Function Types, Parameters, Overloads, and Callable APIs
modules.description: Refactor status calculations and adapters into callable contracts that preserve inputs, failures, and return relationships. Learners cumulatively produce a callable service transformation toolkit through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model parameter, return, callback, this, and side-effect expectations with readable function types.
Reason about callback parameter compatibility under strictFunctionTypes without accepting unsound narrower handlers.
Model optional, default, rest, tuple-rest, and destructured parameters without ambiguous call shapes.

modules.title: Generics, Constraints, Inference, and Variance
modules.description: Build reusable selectors and repositories that preserve relationships between keys, inputs, outputs, and callbacks. Learners cumulatively produce a relationship-preserving generic data toolkit through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Introduce a type parameter only when multiple positions share a relationship that callers need preserved.
Constrain operations while returning the caller-specific subtype rather than widening to the constraint.
Use keyof and indexed access types to connect property names with their corresponding value types.

modules.title: Classes, Modifiers, Private State, and Abstract Contracts
modules.description: Implement scheduling components with runtime invariants, replaceable policies, and explicit construction rules. Learners cumulatively produce a typed scheduling component library through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish instance-side and static-side class types, fields, methods, and constructor signatures.
Use public, protected, TypeScript private, and JavaScript hash-private fields with their actual compile-time and runtime boundaries.
Use parameter properties only when concise construction does not hide validation or ownership transitions.

modules.title: Type Operators, Mapped Types, Conditional Types, and Templates
modules.description: Derive form, event, and patch contracts from one domain model without duplicating names or accepting impossible states. Learners cumulatively produce a derived contract and event-type library through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use type-context typeof to derive from runtime declarations without confusing it with runtime typeof.
Map over property keys with modifiers, key remapping, and value transforms while preserving intended optionality.
Use distributive and non-distributive conditional types with explicit true and false branch meaning.

modules.title: Discriminated Unions, Exhaustiveness, and Result Models
modules.description: Replace boolean status flags and ambiguous errors with explicit request, success, failure, and cancellation states. Learners cumulatively produce an exhaustive asynchronous state machine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Design variants with stable literal discriminants and variant-specific required data.
Use never assignments or exhaustive helpers to make newly added variants produce actionable diagnostics.
Represent expected domain failure as typed result variants while preserving unexpected exceptions.

modules.title: Modules, Resolution, Packages, and Declaration Files
modules.description: Publish a small typed library whose runtime exports, package metadata, and declaration surface agree in clean consumers. Learners cumulatively produce a consumable ESM package with declaration evidence through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose bundler or NodeNext resolution and trace file, extension, package exports, and type lookup decisions.
Distinguish type-only and value imports and preserve runtime side effects intentionally.
Align package exports, imports, type, declaration paths, runtime format, and supported environments.

modules.title: Strict TSConfig, TypeScript 7 Defaults, and Project Graphs
modules.description: Create a clean multi-package configuration that fails on unsafe boundaries, unused work, and incompatible module assumptions. Learners cumulatively produce a strict TypeScript 7 project configuration through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use strict, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, useUnknownInCatchVariables, and override checks from actual risk.
Account for TypeScript 7 strict, target, module, rootDir, types, and side-effect import defaults without legacy no-op flags.
Predict root files from files, include, exclude, imports, types, and library selection.

modules.title: Runtime Validation, Parsing, and Trust Boundaries
modules.description: Parse API, storage, URL, form, and message values into trustworthy domain records without unsafe casts. Learners cumulatively produce a runtime-validated public data adapter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Receive external JSON, storage, DOM, environment, and message data as unknown until evidence narrows it.
Build parsers that return typed values or structured failure paths rather than booleans that discard evidence.
Handle required, optional, deprecated, defaulted, and versioned fields with changed-schema fixtures.

modules.title: Typed Async Work, Platform APIs, and Cancellation
modules.description: Coordinate public-data requests, cancellation, retries, and UI state across browser and server boundaries. Learners cumulatively produce a typed cancellable data coordinator through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model promise fulfillment values and rejection handling without pretending rejected types are encoded in Promise.
Predict async return inference and retain discriminated result relationships across awaits.
Propagate AbortSignal and distinguish cancellation, timeout, transport, parsing, and domain failures.

modules.title: Behavior Tests, Type Tests, and Safe Refactoring
modules.description: Evolve a public API while behavior, diagnostics, inferred types, and downstream consumers remain intentionally compatible. Learners cumulatively produce a behavior-and-type regression suite through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate runtime behavior tests from compile-time assignability, inference, and expected-error tests.
Write stable rejected-usage tests that fail when diagnostics disappear or move for the wrong reason.
Build changed-schema, invalid-value, async, and clean-consumer fixtures around public boundaries.

modules.title: Performance, Migration, API Design, and Release Evidence
modules.description: Migrate and publish a production-shaped TypeScript 7 library with measurable compiler performance and consumer proof. Learners cumulatively produce a TypeScript 7 migration and release portfolio through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Measure cold and warm checks, project graphs, declarations, memory, and parallelism before changing configuration.
Migrate JavaScript incrementally with checkJs, JSDoc, allowJs, boundary validation, and shrinking suppression budgets.
Remove TypeScript 6 deprecations, compare TS6 and TS7 diagnostics, and document temporary API-tool compatibility.

activities.title: TypeScript, JavaScript, and the TypeScript 7 Toolchain · Contract Lens: Static Runtime Boundary
TypeScript, JavaScript, and the TypeScript 7 Toolchain · Type-System Trace: Erasure Emission
TypeScript, JavaScript, and the TypeScript 7 Toolchain · Contract Lens: Ts7 Native Toolchain
